seo.title: Michigan Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Michigan — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license michigan, dental assistant certification michigan, RDA michigan, DANB CDA michigan, dental assistant radiography michigan

board.name: Michigan Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs, Bureau of Professional Licensing, P.O. Box 30670, Lansing, MI 48909

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant (RDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Michigan.

steps.title: Create an ACA Account
steps.description: Visit Michigan's Accela Citizen Access (ACA) online portal, register for an account, and log in to begin the application process.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Start a 'Health Professions Online Application' for 'Registered Dental Assistant' within the ACA portal. Fill out all required sections accurately, including personal, educational, and examination history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents through the ACA portal, which include official transcripts verifying graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program and proof of passing the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB) Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) examination.

steps.title: Pass the Michigan RDA Examination
steps.description: After submitting your application, you will receive information on how to schedule and pass the Michigan Board of Dentistry RDA examination, which is a required step for registration.

steps.title: Pay Application and Examination Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50.00 and the Michigan RDA Examination fee of $25.00 through the ACA portal as part of your application.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Registration
steps.description: Once all requirements are met, documentation is verified, and exam results are processed, the Michigan Board of Dentistry will review your application for final approval and issue your Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) registration.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Michigan Board of Dentistry by registration.

faqs.question: Does Michigan require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Michigan requires Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Michigan?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Michigan.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Michigan?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Michigan?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Michigan Board of Dentistry by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Michigan
hero.dek: To become a Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) in Michigan, applicants must apply through Michigan's Accela Citizen Access (ACA) online portal, which is managed by the Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA) for the Michigan Board of Dentistry.

official_links.portal_name: Michigan's Accela Citizen Access (ACA) online portal
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Michigan Accela Citizen Access (ACA) online portal. Click on 'Register for an Account' to create your user profile. Once registered, log in to begin a new application for 'Health Professions' and select 'Registered Dental Assistant'.
